title: event-ledger schema — pre-register 11 knowledge.* event types
description: Pre-register 11 new event types in the event-ledger discriminated union as `z.literal()` variants, locking the v2 event vocabulary for rc.2/3/4 work. Each variant only specifies the literal type string + minimal payload skeleton (`{ stable_id, ... }`); rc.2/3/4 implementation tasks will fill payload details. Variants to add: `knowledge_proposed`, `knowledge_promote_started`, `knowledge_promoted`, `knowledge_promote_failed`, `knowledge_layer_changed`, `knowledge_slug_renamed`, `knowledge_demoted`, `knowledge_archived`, `knowledge_archive_attempted`, `knowledge_deferred`, `knowledge_rejected` (11 total). Existing 13 event types from rc.2-prep (commit a85121a) remain unchanged and continue to parse.

files.change: Add 11 z.object variants (one per new event type) to the existing z.discriminatedUnion('event_type', [...]) — each variant: z.object({ event_type: z.literal('knowledge_X'), stable_id: z.string().optional(), timestamp: z.string().datetime(), ...minimal payload skeleton }). Preserve all existing 13 variants. Net: 13 → 24 event types.

Add 11 fixture+parse cases (one per new event type), each demonstrating: (a) minimal valid payload parses; (b) wrong event_type literal rejects. Confirm existing 13 event-type fixtures still parse (regression).

implementation: Read existing schema to absorb the discriminated-union pattern (rc.2-prep already established knowledge_* naming for 4 renamed events)

Define 11 new variants — each as: `z.object({ event_type: z.literal('knowledge_<name>'), stable_id: z.string().optional(), timestamp: z.string().datetime(), reason: z.string().optional(), ...optional context fields })`

Specifically: knowledge_proposed (creates pending), knowledge_promote_started/promoted/promote_failed (2-phase Q5 transactional), knowledge_layer_changed (KT↔KP), knowledge_slug_renamed (id stable, slug changed), knowledge_demoted (maturity ↓), knowledge_archived/archive_attempted (archive + retry), knowledge_deferred (defer with until?), knowledge_rejected (reject with reason)
Insert all 11 into the existing z.discriminatedUnion array preserving alphabetical or grouped ordering convention from existing variants
Update test file: write 11 fixture+parse test cases covering valid payloads + 1 reject case (wrong event_type literal)
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared build` — confirm Zod inference compiles cleanly (no overly broad union type)
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared test` — confirm new tests pass and old ones still pass

reference.pattern: Forward-compat event schema pre-registration via z.literal variants in discriminated union
reference.examples: Follow rc.2-prep TASK-006 pattern (commit a85121a) — same discriminated-union variant addition, same minimal payload skeleton convention

criteria: All 11 new event types are z.literal variants in the discriminated union (verify via test exercising each)
Existing 13 event-type fixtures still parse (regression check)
`pnpm --filter @fabric/shared build` exits 0 — Zod inference compiles, no type errors
`pnpm --filter @fabric/shared test` exits 0 — all 11 new test cases pass + all old tests pass
Total event types in union = 24 (13 existing + 11 new) — verified by counting z.literal occurrences

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: emitting these events from runtime code (rc.2/3/4 implementation will wire emitters); event-payload field details beyond the minimal skeleton (deferred to rc.2/3/4 when each event's emit-site context is concrete); api-contracts schema (TASK-003); fabric-config (TASK-002).

rationale.chosen_approach: Pre-register 11 event types as z.literal variants with minimal payload skeleton; defer payload details to rc.2/3/4 when emit-site context is concrete
alternatives_considered: Land full payload schemas now (premature — emit-site context unknown)
Defer all 11 to rc.2/3/4 as needed (loses pre-lock benefit, fragments schema commits)
Single super-event with type field as data (loses discriminator narrowing)
decision_factors: Pre-lock locks vocabulary BEFORE rc.2 dogfood emits real events
Minimal skeleton avoids speculative payload design
z.literal preserves discriminated-union narrowing for consumers
rationale.tradeoffs: Each event-type variant gets touched twice (once here for skeleton, once in rc.2/3/4 for full payload) — accepted because the alternative is fragmented schema commits across 3 RC cycles

manual_checks: Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared build` — confirm Zod discriminated-union inference compiles
Manually call EventLedgerSchema.parse({event_type:'knowledge_promote_started', stable_id:'KT-D-0001', timestamp:'2026-05-10T...'}) — confirm OK
Eyeball discriminated-union ordering — grouped logically (lifecycle / archive / review)
success_metrics: All 11 new event-type test cases pass
All existing 13 event-type tests still pass
Discriminated-union narrowing works in TS consumer code
unit: knowledge_deferred parses with until field optional
